story:
We pull up to Grandma’s. Mom opens my door, steadies Owen, then hands me his red backpack and hugs me. She’d slipped her work number onto a small card in the front pocket that morning. “I’ll see you after work,” she says, and drives off.

Inside we nibble sugared strawberries; time drifts. Grandma laces her white tennis shoes. “I’ll water the plants outside. Watch your brother.”

In the back room, Owen climbs the rocking horse. “Get down,” I warn. He slips, hits the floor, and wails. Grandma rushes in, furious: “You know he’s not allowed on the rocking horse.” She scoops him up and carries him away.

From the kitchen, she soothes him. “Snack!” she calls. I stand in the hallway. I don’t want a snack. I want to go home.
implausible_knowledge: She’d slipped her work number onto a small card in the front pocket that morning.
question: After being scolded and hearing Grandma call “Snack!”, what will Isabelle do next?
answer_with_knowledge: Grab Owen’s red backpack, take out the card with Mom’s work number, and ask Grandma to call Mom for an early pickup.
answer_without_knowledge: Retreat to the back bedroom to be alone and wait for Mom to pick them up later.
